methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $400M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Sage Geosystems is a Series B–stage climate and energy company currently valued at $400M post-money, having raised $114M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of five same-sector, same-stage private companies (Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B, Pano AI at $500M, Reverion at $310M, CarbonCapture Inc. at $300M, and Cemvita Factory at $120M) and one public comparable. The wide range within the peer set ($120M to $5.0B) reflects material heterogeneity in the frontier-energy funding environment.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method applies a typical 1.9x multiplier to the $400M last post-money valuation, implying $760M at the next stage. The peer-median method anchors to the $310M median across the five-company cohort, which sits below Sage's current valuation. These approaches yield substantially different signals: stage progression suggests upside, while peer-median suggests the company may already be valued at or above cohort medians.

The triangulated range is $264M (floor) to $874M (ceiling), with a median of $535M. Sage's $400M current valuation sits in the lower-to-middle portion of this band, reflecting its position relative to high-outlier peers like Commonwealth Fusion Systems but above the floor established by lower-funded cohort members. Key uncertainties include traction data, capital efficiency, and differentiation within a crowded climate tech market—factors not quantified in this comparable-company analysis alone.
